_publications._publicationTitle: Complex splicing pattern generates great diversity in human NF1 transcripts.

_contextSnippet: The SSPNN program gave the exons that can become skipped a significantly lower score (0.63, p = 0.03) at the acceptor site, compared to the mean NF1 acceptor score for all NF1 exons (0.81).
resourceName: Splice Site Prediction by Neural Network (SSPNN)
details: Successfully calculated splice site strength scores for all constitutive and alternative exons, identifying that exons prone to skipping had significantly lower acceptor scores (0.63 vs 0.81, p=0.03)
